title: Apricity, The Quiet Return
caption: A poem on finding small warmth in the cold seasons of memory.
artworkAlt: A solitary person walking along a winter road toward the light
body:
There are winters that belong to the earth and winters that belong to us.

The first arrive with frost and shortened days.
The fields forget their colours.
The rivers forget their movement.
Even the sky grows tired of its own grey.

The second arrive without weather.
They come as silence after a decision,
as the long echo of a door closed somewhere in the past,
as years spent wandering the cold corridors
of a question that will not answer.
